questions.question: What does the term 'Data Communication' primarily refer to?
options: Exchange of data between two or more networked devices
Storing data on a hard drive
questions.explanation: Data communication refers to the exchange of data between two or more networked or connected devices.
questions.subject: Computer Science

questions.question: Which component of data communication is the information to be communicated?
questions.explanation: Message is the information to be communicated. It may include text, number, images, audio, or video.
questions.subject: Computer Science

questions.question: Which device is responsible for sending the data message?
questions.explanation: Sender is a device that sends the data message.
questions.subject: Computer Science

questions.question: What is the physical path over which a message travels from sender to receiver called?
questions.explanation: The communication channel is the physical path (wired or wireless) through which the message travels from sender to receiver.
questions.subject: Computer Science

questions.question: What is the set of rules that governs data communication called?
questions.explanation: A protocol is a set of rules that governs data communication.
questions.subject: Computer Science

questions.question: In data communication, the 'Receiver' is the device that:
questions.explanation: Receiver is the device that receives the data message.
questions.subject: Computer Science

questions.question: What does 'Bandwidth' represent in data communication?
options: The physical length of the cable
The maximum amount of data that can be sent over a channel in a given unit of time
The number of devices connected to a network
The speed of the sender's processor
questions.explanation: Bandwidth is the maximum amount of data that can be sent over a specific communication channel in a given unit of time.
questions.subject: Computer Science

questions.question: What is the unit of bandwidth in digital systems?
questions.explanation: In digital systems, bandwidth is measured in bits per second (bps).
questions.subject: Computer Science

questions.question: How many bits per second is 1 Mbps equal to?
questions.explanation: 1 Mbps is equal to 1,000,000 bits per second (10^6 bps).
questions.subject: Computer Science

questions.question: What is the actual speed at which data is transferred over a communication channel called?
questions.explanation: Data transfer rate is the actual speed of data transfer over a communication channel.
questions.subject: Computer Science

questions.question: In which communication mode is data travel unidirectional?
questions.explanation: In simplex mode, the communication is unidirectional.
questions.subject: Computer Science

questions.question: A keyboard or a traditional television broadcast is an example of which communication mode?
questions.explanation: Keyboard and traditional monitors/TVs are examples of simplex devices.
questions.subject: Computer Science

questions.question: Which communication mode allows data flow in both directions, but only one direction at a time?
questions.explanation: In half-duplex mode, each station can both transmit and receive, but not at the same time.
questions.subject: Computer Science

questions.question: A Walkie-Talkie is a common example of which communication mode?
questions.explanation: Walkie-talkie is an example of a half-duplex device.
questions.subject: Computer Science

questions.question: Which communication mode allows simultaneous data flow in both directions?
questions.explanation: In full-duplex mode, both stations can transmit and receive simultaneously.
questions.subject: Computer Science

questions.question: A mobile phone conversation is an example of which communication mode?
questions.explanation: Mobile phones are examples of full-duplex devices.
questions.subject: Computer Science

questions.question: Which switching technique establishes a dedicated path between two nodes for the entire duration of the communication?
questions.explanation: In circuit switching, a dedicated path is established between the sender and the receiver.
questions.subject: Computer Science

questions.question: What is the main drawback of circuit switching?
options: Inefficient use of channel capacity because the path is reserved even when no data is being sent
It is very slow for voice calls
questions.explanation: The main drawback is that the channel is reserved even when no data is being sent, leading to inefficiency.
questions.subject: Computer Science

questions.question: Which switching technique divides data into small blocks with headers containing source and destination addresses?
questions.explanation: In packet switching, the message is divided into small blocks called packets, each with a header for routing.
questions.subject: Computer Science

questions.question: What happens if a packet arrives at an intermediate node and the outgoing link is busy in packet switching?
options: The node waits and stores the packet until the link is free
The packet is sent back to the sender
The packet is rerouted to a random node
questions.explanation: If the outgoing link is busy, the node stores the packet in a queue and waits until the link is free (Store and Forward).
questions.subject: Computer Science

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a type of wired (guided) transmission media?
questions.explanation: Infrared is a wireless (unguided) transmission medium.
questions.subject: Computer Science

questions.question: Which cable consists of two copper wires twisted together to reduce electromagnetic interference?
questions.explanation: A twisted pair cable consists of two copper wires twisted together in a spiral to reduce interference.
questions.subject: Computer Science

questions.question: What are the two types of twisted pair cables?
questions.explanation: Twisted pair cables are classified into Unshielded Twisted Pair (UTP) and Shielded Twisted Pair (STP).
questions.subject: Computer Science

questions.question: Which type of cable is commonly used for cable TV and high-speed internet because of its ability to carry multiple channels?
questions.explanation: Coaxial cable is widely used for cable TV and high-speed data communications.
questions.subject: Computer Science

questions.question: Which transmission medium carries data as pulses of light through glass or plastic strands?
questions.explanation: Optical fiber carries data in the form of light pulses through glass or plastic fibers.
questions.subject: Computer Science

questions.question: What is a major advantage of optical fiber over copper cables?
options: It is unaffected by electromagnetic interference (EMI)
It is easier to bend and route
questions.explanation: Optical fiber is immune to electromagnetic interference because it transmits light, not electrical signals.
questions.subject: Computer Science

questions.question: Which wireless technology is used for short-distance data exchange between fixed and mobile devices using radio waves?
questions.explanation: Bluetooth is used for short-distance communication between devices like mobile phones and laptops.
questions.subject: Computer Science

questions.question: What is the range of frequency for radio waves?
questions.explanation: Radio waves have a frequency range from 3 KHz to 300 GHz.
questions.subject: Computer Science

questions.question: Which technology allows local area networking of devices using radio waves, typically used in homes and cafes?
questions.explanation: Wi-Fi technology allows networking of devices through radio waves and is commonly used in hotspots.
questions.subject: Computer Science

questions.question: What are high-frequency radio waves in the range of 300 MHz to 300 GHz called?
questions.explanation: Microwaves are radio waves with frequencies between 300 MHz and 300 GHz.
questions.subject: Computer Science

questions.question: Which wireless medium is commonly used in TV remote controls and has a very short range?
questions.explanation: Infrared communication is used over short distances, such as in TV remote controls.
questions.subject: Computer Science

questions.question: Which mobile telecommunication generation introduced broadband speeds for the first time?
questions.explanation: 3G technology introduced broadband speeds and enabled video calls and mobile internet.
questions.subject: Computer Science

questions.question: What is the primary feature that distinguishes 4G (LTE) from 3G?
options: It is only for voice calls
Higher data speeds and improved network capacity for high-definition streaming
questions.explanation: 4G (LTE) provides significantly higher data speeds and better capacity for streaming and online gaming.
questions.subject: Computer Science

questions.question: Which mobile generation is expected to offer 'ultra-low latency' and support the Internet of Things (IoT)?
questions.explanation: 5G is expected to offer extremely high speeds, low latency, and massive connectivity for IoT.
questions.subject: Computer Science

questions.question: What does HTTP stand for?
questions.explanation: HTTP stands for Hypertext Transfer Protocol.
questions.subject: Computer Science

questions.question: Which protocol is used to transfer files over the internet between a client and a server?
questions.explanation: FTP (File Transfer Protocol) is used for transferring files between devices over a network.
questions.subject: Computer Science

questions.question: Which protocol allows users to send and receive emails?
questions.explanation: SMTP (Simple Mail Transfer Protocol) is used for sending and receiving emails.
questions.subject: Computer Science

questions.question: What does PPP stand for in networking?
questions.explanation: PPP stands for Point-to-Point Protocol.
questions.subject: Computer Science

questions.question: Which protocol defines how two devices authenticate each other and establish a direct link to exchange data?
questions.explanation: PPP defines how two devices authenticate and establish a link for data exchange.
questions.subject: Computer Science

questions.question: What does TCP/IP stand for?
questions.explanation: TCP/IP stands for Transmission Control Protocol/Internet Protocol.
questions.subject: Computer Science

questions.question: Which part of TCP/IP is responsible for breaking data into packets and ensuring they are correctly reassembled at the destination?
questions.explanation: TCP breaks the message into packets and reassembles them at the receiver end, checking for errors.
questions.subject: Computer Science

questions.question: Which part of TCP/IP handles the addressing and routing of packets through the network?
questions.explanation: IP is responsible for addressing and routing packets to the correct destination.
questions.subject: Computer Science

questions.question: What is the system that translates human-readable domain names (like www.google.com) into IP addresses?
questions.explanation: DNS (Domain Name System) translates domain names into numerical IP addresses.
questions.subject: Computer Science

questions.question: In the client-server model, what is the 'Client'?
options: A user or machine that requests a service from a server
questions.explanation: A client is a host or machine that requests a service by a server in the network.
questions.subject: Computer Science

questions.question: What is the frequency range of Infrared waves?
questions.explanation: Infrared waves have frequencies from 300 GHz to 400 THz.
questions.subject: Computer Science

questions.question: Which cable consists of a solid wire core, a layer of insulation, a metallic braid, and a plastic cover?
questions.explanation: Coaxial cable has a central copper conductor, an insulating layer, a metallic shield (braid), and an outer jacket.
questions.subject: Computer Science

questions.question: What is the main advantage of unguided (wireless) media over guided media?
options: Mobility and flexibility for the user
questions.explanation: Wireless media provide the advantage of mobility and ease of installation in difficult terrains.
questions.subject: Computer Science

questions.question: Which of the following is a unit of data transfer rate?
questions.explanation: Data transfer rate is measured in bits per second (bps), Kbps, Mbps, etc.
questions.subject: Computer Science

questions.question: In the TCP/IP suite, 'HTTP' is primarily used at which layer level functionality?
questions.explanation: HTTP is an application-level protocol used by the web browser to access websites.
questions.subject: Computer Science

questions.question: Bluetooth technology is primarily intended for:
options: Connecting devices over a long range
questions.explanation: Bluetooth is a short-distance wireless communication technology for mobile devices.
questions.subject: Computer Science
